title: What is AI, really?
concept: AI is software that learns patterns from data — not a conscious robot that thinks like a human.

cards.title: You already use AI every day
cards.body: You just call it "my phone." Unlocking with your face, autocorrect, photo search, song recommendations — all AI. Not science fiction; already in your pocket.

cards.title: So what IS AI?
cards.body: Artificial Intelligence is software that does tasks normally needing human intelligence — recognizing a face, understanding a sentence, recommending a song — by learning patterns from data instead of following hand-written rules.

cards.title: How a toddler learns "dog"
cards.body:
A child doesn't memorize a rulebook ("4 legs + fur + tail = dog"). They see many dogs and learn the pattern.

AI learns the exact same way: from examples, not rules.

cards.title: Your photos app, in action
cards.body:
When Google Photos finds every picture of your dog, nobody wrote "if fur and four legs → dog."

The system learned "dog" by studying millions of labeled photos.

cards.title: The myth: a thinking robot
cards.body:
The sci-fi image — a machine that thinks, feels, and wants things — is NOT today's AI.

Real AI has no understanding, no feelings. It is extremely good pattern-matching at massive scale.

left.text:
Conscious sci-fi robot
Thinks & feels like a person

exercises.prompt: True or false: AI is a robot that thinks and feels like a human.
exercises.explain: False. Today's AI has no feelings or real understanding — it finds patterns in data. The "thinking robot" is science fiction.

options.text: It's handed a rulebook for every possible situation
It learns patterns from lots of examples
It connects to the internet to look up every answer

exercises.explain: AI learns patterns from data and examples — that's the core idea that separates it from ordinary rule-following software.

exercises.sentence: Instead of following hand-written rules, AI ___ patterns from data.
exercises.explain: AI learns. Learning from data — rather than being told every rule — is what makes something "AI."

exercises.prompt: Which one is AI at work?

options.text: Your keyboard suggesting the next word as you type
A light turning on when you flip a switch

exercises.explain: The calculator and light switch follow fixed rules. Next-word suggestion learned from huge amounts of text — that's AI.

exercises.prompt: Your photos app groups every photo of your cat automatically. How?

options.text: A programmer wrote exact rules describing a cat
It asked you to tag every single photo first
It learned "cat" from seeing huge numbers of example images

exercises.explain: Like the toddler learning "dog," the app learned from many labeled examples — not a hand-written rulebook.

exercises.prompt: A friend says: "AI is basically a super-smart robot brain that already understands everything like a person." Your best response?

options.text: "Correct — modern AI thinks just like us."
"Not quite — AI learns patterns from massive data, but doesn't truly understand or feel."
"AI is just a calculator with a screen."

exercises.explain: AI is powerful pattern-matching at scale — genuinely useful, but not conscious understanding. You just nailed the #1 misconception about AI! 🎉
